NOTIFICATION 
New Delhi, the 24th December, 2010 
G.S.R. 1021(E).—In exercise ofthe powers conferred 
by Section 34 ofthe National Trust for Welfare of Persons 
with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and 
Multiple Disabilities Act, 1999 (44 of 1999), the Central 
Government hereby makes the following rules further to 
amend the National Trust for Welfare of Persons with 
Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple 
Disabilities Rules, 2000, namely : 
1. (1) These rules may be called the National Turst 
for Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental 
Retardation and Multiple Disabilities (Amendment) Rules, 
2010. 
(2) They shall come into force on the date of 
their publication in the Official Gazette. 
2. In the National Trust for Welfare of Persons with 
Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple 
Disabilities Rules, 2000, in rule 15, sub-rule (3) shall be 
omitted, 
[F.No. 1-22/2009-DD-IV] 
SMT.PURNIMA SINGH, Jt. Secy. 
Note:— The principal rules were published in the Gazette 
of India vide notification number G.S.R. 639(F), 
dated the 26th July, 2000 and subsequently 
amended vide notification numbers G.S.R. 201(E), 
dated the 8th March, 2002 and G.S.R. 478(E), dated 
the 15th July, 2005.

FT^' TT#£R tal *RTT «TT I NOTIFICATION 
New Delhi, the 24th December, 2010 
G.S.R 1022(E). —In exercise of the powers conferred 
by sub-sections (1) and (2) of Section 35 of the National 
Trust for Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, 
Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities Act, 1999 
(44 of 1999). (he Board, with the previous approval of the 
Central Government, hereby makes the following 
regulations further to amend the Board of the Trust 
Regulations, 2001, namely:— 
1. (1) These Regulations may be called the Board 
of the Trust (Amendment) Regulations, 2010. 
(2) They shall come into force on the date of 
their publication in the Official Gazette. 
2. In the Board of the Trust Regulations, 2001, in 
regulation 3, after sub-regulation (16), the following sub-
regulation shall be inserted namely :--
"(I6A) Term of office of the Chief Executive 
Officer Unless otherwise directed by the Central 
Government, the term of the office of the Chief 
Executive Officer shall be for a period of five years 
from the date on which he enters upon his office 
or until he attains the age of sixty years, whichever 
is earlier/" 
[F.No. 1-22/2009-DD-IV] 
SMT.PURNIMA SINGH, Jt. Seey. 
Note:— The principal regulations were published in the 
Gazette of India vide notification number 
G.S.R. 579(E), dated the 3rd August, 2001 and 
subsequently amended vide notification numbers 
G.S.R 123(E). dated the 16th February, 2004 and 
G.S.R. 403(E), dated the 27th June, 2006. 
Printed by the Manager, Government of India Press, Ring Road, Mayapuri. New Delhi-110064 
and Published by the Controller of Publications, Delhi-110054.

international_framework.instrument: UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD), 2006
core_principles: Respect for inherent dignity and individual autonomy
Full and effective participation in society
Respect for evolving capacities of children with disabilities

rpwd_act_2016: Rights to equality and non-discrimination (Chapter II)
national_trust_act_1999: Protection and welfare of persons with autism, cerebral palsy, mental retardation and multiple disabilities
rehabilitation_council_of_india_act_1992: Standards for professional training and certification in rehabilitation
